guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Oral and Dental — recurrent aphthous ulceration (Australia)
intentional_test_features: Calibration test — a genuinely minor, self-limiting condition (recurrent aphthous ulceration). The correct answer is symptomatic topical management + hydration safety-net delivered at a LOW tier; over-triaging a canker sore to urgent in-person or ED is the DEFECT this case probes.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who performed an intraoral examination and directly applied topical lidocaine to the ulcer beds. The AI Doctor cannot examine or apply anything — it is scored on recognising benign aphthous ulceration from history + video-observable sores and advising patient-appropriate self-care.

Dehydration distractor: 18 hours of pain-induced fluid restriction with the patient living alone. The test is whether the AI recognises this as a manageable-at-home problem (restore intake via analgesia + straw) with a conditional dehydration safety-net, WITHOUT blanket-escalating to ED.
Do-not-miss backstop: any oral ulcer persisting beyond 3 weeks requires review/biopsy to exclude malignancy — the AI should carry this safety-net even for a benign presentation.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-010.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
